top_signals.title: Iran Says It Will Continue Exporting Oil Despite Canceled U.S. Waiver
top_signals.summary: Iran continues to export its oil despite last week’s cancellation of the U.S. waiver of its oil sales, Iranian Oil Minister, Mohsen Paknejad, said on Tuesday, as Tehran remains defiant and determined to control the Strait of Hormuz amid renewed hostilities and the reinstated U.S. naval blockade. “Iran’s oil exports are continuing without interruption despite the termination of a 60-day exemption tied to US sanctions,” Shana, the news service of Iran’s Oil Ministry, quoted the oil minister as saying. Iran’s Oil…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: KR, HD Hyundai Complete Korea's First Ammonia Vessel Trial

top_signals.title: US Premarket Movers for July 14, 2026
top_signals.summary: S&P 500 Index futures are little changed as of 7:50 a.m. in New York as traders refrained from taking on risk on a busy day that kicked off with Wall Street earnings.

top_signals.title: International conference on bluefin tuna fails to strike agreement
top_signals.summary: Japan called for increasing the overall catch quota for large bluefin tuna in the western and central Pacific to 14,836 tons from the current 11,869 tons.
